description: Clean baseline for solver4 32x8x10 autoresearch lane: 4 fixed gamma=0 seeds at 60s each, after archiving stale solver3-era autoresearch artifacts.

asi.hypothesis: Baseline restored strong heuristic branch on a clean solver4-specific 32x8x10 multiseed lane.
asi.baseline_shape: Current branch already solves 32x8x9 but plateaus on 32x8x10; seed_3 is strongest and seed_4 is weakest.
asi.next_action_hint: Target solver4 search-basin quality on 32x8x10 via diversification, plateau escape, or conflict-concentration handling without changing lane semantics.

description: Make solver4 breakouts conflict-guided by choosing max-conflict weeks instead of uniformly random weeks.

asi.hypothesis: Breakouts were wasting perturbations on clean weeks; focusing them on max-conflict weeks should improve weak seeds.
asi.learned_tradeoff: This improved the 4-seed mean and worst seed materially, but eliminated the previous seed_3 spike (5094/466) and collapsed all best-conflict scores to 64.
asi.basin_shape: The change seems to homogenize outcomes: weaker seeds improved more than the strongest seed.
asi.next_action_hint: Try preserving conflict-guided breakouts while restoring more upside via stronger intra-week perturbation diversity or aspiration/tie-break behavior within the focused weeks.

description: Keep conflict-guided breakout weeks and also target conflicted positions within those weeks during random breakout swaps.

asi.hypothesis: Focused breakout weeks still left random swaps on clean positions; restricting swap endpoints to conflicted positions should improve perturbation efficiency.
asi.key_result: Large mean improvement to 5136 with all four seeds converging to the same 464-contact basin.
asi.tradeoff: This removed both the weak 452-contact tail and the previous 466-contact spike; the search now strongly favors a stable 464-contact plateau.
asi.next_action_hint: Push past the 464-contact plateau by changing the move neighborhood or tie-break objective, not by further homogenizing breakout selection.

description: Tie-break equal-conflict local-search swaps by lower repeat_excess before lexicographic order; improved three seeds from 464 to 474 contacts and cut mean_final_score from 5136.0 to 4978.5.

asi.hypothesis: Among swaps with the same paper conflict-position score, preferring lower repeat_excess should steer solver4 toward better canonical-quality basins on 32x8x10 without changing the primary move objective.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe whether the remaining seed-3 plateau can be broken by conflict-aware selection among equal-repeat-excess swaps or by stronger plateau escapes after reaching the 24-conflict basin.

description: Among equal-conflict and equal-repeat-excess swaps, prefer moves that reduce the worst per-week conflict concentration before lexicographic tie-breaking; this solved two seeds to 480 contacts and improved mean_final_score from 4978.5 to 4968.0.

asi.hypothesis: When conflict-position and repeat-excess tie, preferring swaps that lower the maximum weekly conflict concentration should help solver4 finish concentrated basins on 32x8x10.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate why seeds 3 and 4 remain stuck at the 64-conflict basin; likely next leverage is stronger diversification or breakout targeting conditioned on repeated failure to enter the low-conflict basin.

description: Fix the Social Golfer benchmark encoding to use meet-at-most-once semantics (`RepeatEncounter.max_allowed_encounters = 1`) and tighten solver4 gating to require that encoding; under the corrected canonical scorer, two seeds now score 0 and the new baseline mean_final_score is 88.0.

asi.hypothesis: The Social Golfer benchmark cases were misconfigured with max_allowed_encounters=0, so correcting them to meet-at-most-once (=1) and matching solver4 gating should make perfect zero-repeat schedules score 0 canonically.
asi.target_change: Re-initialized autoresearch because the benchmark question changed from the misconfigured encoding to the intended meet-at-most-once semantics.
asi.next_action_hint: Resume search-quality work on the corrected lane by targeting the remaining two 176-score seeds without perturbing the now-correct canonical scoring semantics.

description: Tried a deeper tie-break chain that, after conflict positions / repeat_excess / max weekly conflict, prefers lower squared weekly conflict mass before lexicographic order. It regressed the corrected meet-at-most-once lane badly: no solved seeds, mean_final_score worsened from 88 to 194.

asi.hypothesis: Among swaps still tied on conflict_positions, repeat_excess, and max weekly conflict, preferring lower squared weekly conflict mass might smooth the remaining 176-score plateaus into solvable basins.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra tie-break overfit the local-search ordering and destroyed the strong solved-seed behavior on the corrected lane, worsening mean_final_score from 88 to 194 and reducing solved_runs from 2 to 0.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this square-sum tie-break and try a less invasive direction, likely in breakout/restart behavior conditioned on being stuck in the 176 basin rather than adding another deterministic local-search ordering layer.

name: solver4 p4 stretch bundle quality (32x8x10 multiseed + larger p4 cases)

description: Attempted to rerun the expanded p=4 stretch autoresearch baseline, but the wrapper failed immediately with a permission error: tools/autoresearch/solver4-8x4x10/autoresearch.sh was not executable.

asi.hypothesis: The solver2 cleanup was complete, so the expanded benchmark baseline should run cleanly.
asi.rollback_reason: The benchmark wrapper itself was not executable, so the run never started.
asi.next_action_hint: Restore execute permission on tools/autoresearch/solver4-8x4x10/autoresearch.sh and rerun the baseline.

description: Retried the expanded baseline after restoring execute permissions, but the wrapper still exited 1 before emitting a run report. The captured log only contained compiler warnings, so the harness is still failing before benchmark execution.

asi.hypothesis: After fixing the execute bit, the wrapper should launch the expanded benchmark suite and produce the first valid baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: The wrapper still exited without a run report, so there is still a harness failure and no baseline metrics.

error_details.observed_log: compiler warnings only; no 'Run report:' line emitted

asi.next_action_hint: Run the benchmark command directly outside the wrapper to determine whether cargo run returns nonzero despite successful compilation or whether the wrapper’s report-path extraction is the failing step.

description: Established the first valid baseline for the expanded p=4 stretch autoresearch bundle after fixing the non-canonical suite policy in the benchmark manifest. The 20-case jobs=4 lane now benchmarks 32x8x10 plus 36x9x10, 36x9x11, 40x10x10, and 40x10x11 with 4 seeds each.

asi.hypothesis: The expanded 4-seed-per-case p=4 stretch bundle should now run cleanly and establish a trustworthy new baseline for solver4 quality work.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize the remaining unsolved basins, especially 32x8x10 seeds 3-4 and 36x9x11, while preserving the already-solved 32x8x10 and 40x10x10 seeds.

description: Tried replacing the random two-swap breakout with a solver3-inspired structured week repair: rebuild one max-conflict week greedily from the current cross-week partnership state, and only fall back to random swaps if the rebuilt week is unchanged. This generalized perturbation looked attractive in theory but regressed the p=4 bundle badly.

asi.hypothesis: A structured week-rebuild breakout should generalize better than blind swaps by repairing one whole max-conflict week using the same freedom-aware constructor under the current cross-week constraints.
asi.rollback_reason: The stronger destroy-repair breakout over-intensified deterministic basins, eliminated all solved runs, and worsened the bundle mean from 111.65 to 128.4.
asi.dead_end: Whole-week greedy repair is too strong and too deterministic as the default breakout operator for this lane.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout lightweight and instead explore a smaller generalization from solver3, such as using incident-burden information to choose breakout positions or widening the selected-swap neighborhood only around the highest-burden repeated pairs.

description: Tried a lighter solver3-inspired generalization: keep the existing breakout operator, but bias breakout position choice toward the highest-incident conflict slots within the selected max-conflict week instead of choosing uniformly among conflicted slots. This was more robust than whole-week repair, but still regressed the expanded p=4 bundle relative to baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A smaller solver3-style guidance signal should help more generally than the failed whole-week rebuild: if breakout swaps target the highest-incident conflict slots inside the worst week, they may escape heavy corridors without collapsing solved seeds.
asi.rollback_reason: The incident-burden breakout targeting was directionally saner than week-repair but still worsened the bundle mean from 111.65 to 115.0, while reducing solved runs from 3 to 2.
asi.learned_tradeoff: More aggressive burden targeting helps some hard seeds and larger cases, but it destabilizes already-solved easy seeds; the breakout operator is sensitive to over-guidance.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing breakout targeting and instead explore guidance inside move selection, such as widening the neighborhood or candidate ordering around the highest-burden repeated pairs while leaving breakout stochasticity mostly intact.

description: Tried a solver3-style diversification inside move selection rather than breakout: when the search has already stagnated, choose randomly among swaps tied on the main preview tuple (conflict positions, repeat excess, max weekly conflict) instead of always taking the lexicographically smallest one. This preserved 3 solved runs but still slightly regressed the expanded bundle mean.

asi.hypothesis: A little solver3-like stochasticity inside the tied move set should diversify plateau traversal without oversteering breakout behavior: only randomize among fully tied top swaps after the search has already stopped improving for a while.
asi.rollback_reason: The tied-swap randomization kept the run broadly competitive but still worsened the mean from 111.65 to 114.55, so it did not clear the primary metric.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Injecting randomness into tied local-search moves is less destructive than breakout over-guidance, but it still perturbs easy solved seeds enough to lose bundle mean.
asi.next_action_hint: Resume from the reverted baseline and look for a more structural but still general move-quality signal, likely something that helps distinguish tied swaps by future repairability instead of adding more randomness.

description: Tried a solver3-inspired move-quality signal instead of extra randomness: among swaps tied on conflict positions, repeat excess, and max weekly conflict, prefer moves that reduce the worst repeated-pair burden among the pairs touched by the swap. This helped some larger p=4 cases, but the bundle mean still regressed slightly because 32x8x10 seeds 3-4 stayed stuck and 36x9x11 seed 2 worsened.

asi.hypothesis: A more structural solver3-style signal may beat extra randomness: among otherwise tied swaps, favor those that locally reduce the worst repeated-pair corridor touched by the move, which should improve future repairability across all pure-SGP cases.
asi.rollback_reason: The changed-pair burden tie-break was plausible and mildly helpful on 36x9x10 / 40x10x11, but it still raised the overall mean from 111.65 to 113.1 and badly worsened one 36x9x11 seed.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Pair-burden-aware tie-breaking can help some mid-hard cases, but as a global ordering signal it still destabilizes the hardest bundle member more than it helps.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the baseline local-search ordering and try a truly orthogonal diversification mechanism next, likely a very lightweight elite-week transplant or another constructor-side diversity change that does not perturb solved seeds as aggressively.

description: Tried a lightweight elite-week transplant inspired by solver3 donor-session ideas: on breakout, if the current schedule differs from the incumbent best schedule in the chosen max-conflict week, copy that week from the incumbent instead of doing two blind random swaps. This was far too destabilizing for the bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A donor-session-style breakout may be a safer orthogonal diversification operator than greedy week repair: transplant a single elite week from the incumbent best schedule only when the current schedule has drifted in that same max-conflict week.
asi.rollback_reason: The elite-week transplant made the bundle much worse, dropping solved runs from 3 to 1 and raising mean_final_score from 111.65 to 132.05.
asi.dead_end: Whole-week state transplants are still too coarse for solver4's current basin geometry, even when the donor week comes from the incumbent best schedule rather than the constructor.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid coarse week-level perturbations. Next promising direction remains constructor-side or move-ranking diversity that changes basin entry more gently, not breakout replacement.

description: Tried a constructor-side dual-seed idea: evaluate both the normal greedy week order and the reversed week order, then start local search from whichever initial schedule has the lower initial paper objective. In practice this was effectively a wash against the current baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A gentle constructor-side diversity change might help without destabilizing solved seeds: build both forward-week and reverse-week greedy schedules, then keep the better initial basin before local search.
asi.rollback_reason: The dual week-order seeding produced effectively the same bundle quality as the existing baseline, so it did not justify changing the algorithm.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this as a neutral result and continue exploring other constructor-side diversity or finer-grained local-search guidance; do not keep the extra complexity for a no-op.

description: Tried a solver3-style repeat-guidance refinement inside local-search tie-breaking: among swaps already tied on conflict positions and repeat excess, prefer the move that leaves fewer distinct active repeated pairs before using weekly conflict concentration and lexicographic order. This improved one 32x8x10 seed and one 36x9x11 seed, but still regressed the bundle mean slightly because other hard seeds worsened.

asi.hypothesis: Among equal-conflict, equal-repeat swaps, favoring lower active repeated-pair count should improve future repairability more generally than the previous max-changed-pair-burden tie-break.
asi.rollback_reason: The active-repeated-pairs tie-break helped some individual seeds but still raised the bundle mean from 111.65 to 112.9, mainly because 36x9x11 seed 2 blew up to 450 and 32x8x10 seed 4 stayed stuck.
asi.learned_tradeoff: This signal is more promising than the previous pair-burden max tie-break, but it still oversteers some hard large-case basins.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this tie-break and try a different gentle constructor-side diversity idea or a more localized repeat-guidance signal that only activates on severely stagnating runs.

description: Tried a constructor-side dual seed for p=4: build both the current whole-group size-4 initializer and the older pairwise size-4 initializer, then keep whichever initial schedule has the lower initial paper objective before local search. In practice this behaved as another neutral no-op versus baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A more structurally distinct constructor alternative may provide generic diversity without changing benchmark semantics: build both whole-group and pairwise p=4 greedy schedules, then start local search from the better initial basin.
asi.rollback_reason: The dual whole-group/pairwise constructor choice did not move the bundle at all relative to baseline, so it adds complexity without measurable benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep looking for gentler but more meaningfully different diversity sources; simple best-of-two constructor variants appear too correlated with the current baseline basin.

description: Conditioned a solver3-inspired repeat-guidance signal on true stagnation: among swaps tied on conflict positions and repeat excess, prefer fewer distinct active repeated pairs only after the search has already stalled for 2+ iterations, while preserving the original ordering earlier. This improved the p=4 stretch bundle substantially.

asi.hypothesis: A gentle repeat-guidance signal should only influence hard plateau states, not easy early descents: activate active-repeated-pair minimization only after 2+ non-improving iterations, while keeping the baseline swap ordering before that.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Conditional guidance helps the broader bundle without destabilizing the already-solved easy 32x8x10 seeds, but it still leaves the hardest 32x8x10 seeds 3-4 untouched and one 36x9x11 seed remains quite weak.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this guarded repeat-guidance path and next focus on constructor-side or stagnation-only diversification that specifically helps the remaining 32x8x10 hard seeds without losing the new gains on larger p=4 cases.

description: Activated the new active-repeated-pair guidance one step earlier in stagnation (after 1 non-improving iteration instead of 2). That overreached: it hurt the broader p=4 stretch bundle and reduced solved runs.

asi.hypothesis: If the guarded active-repeated-pair signal was helpful only late, perhaps turning it on slightly earlier after the first non-improving iteration would help plateau cases sooner without disturbing easy descents too much.
asi.rollback_reason: Earlier activation regressed mean_final_score from 101.95 to 112.2 and reduced solved_runs from 5 to 4, so the guidance becomes too aggressive when it starts after only one stagnant iteration.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The active-repeated-pair signal is useful as a late tie-breaker but harmful when it starts influencing medium-stagnation states; the threshold itself matters materially.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the >=2 stagnation threshold and explore orthogonal generic ideas next, likely gentle constructor diversity or a different late-only signal that targets hard cases without broadening influence earlier in the search.

description: Tried a more localized late-stage repeat-guidance tie-break: after true stagnation, among swaps already tied on conflict, repeat excess, and active repeated pairs, prefer the move with lower max incident burden on the changed positions. This regressed badly.

asi.hypothesis: A very local late-only incident-bucket signal might be safer than broader ordering changes: after 2+ stagnant iterations, break ties by preferring swaps that leave a lower worst incident burden on the positions they actually touch.
asi.rollback_reason: This signal regressed mean_final_score from 101.95 to 122.6, crashed one formerly solved 32x8x10 seed to 256, and worsened the hard 36x9x11 seeds.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Even when delayed until real stagnation, additional incident-bucket guidance appears too distortive; local-search tie-breaking is now easy to over-steer on this bundle.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop adding more late-stage local-search ranking criteria for now. Shift the next experiment toward constructor-side diversity or another basin-entry change that stays generic and leaves the kept late repeat-guidance untouched.

description: Tried constructor-side symmetry seeding: build the greedy initializer under a deterministic seed-derived person relabeling, then map back before local search. This changed basins but regressed the bundle overall.

asi.hypothesis: The current constructor may still be trapped by arbitrary person-id tie bias, so a deterministic seed-derived person relabeling could create meaningfully different but still fully honest initial basins without spending extra search budget.
asi.rollback_reason: Seed-derived person relabeling regressed mean_final_score from 101.95 to 117.7 and reduced solved_runs back to 3, with a major hit on 36x9x11 seed 1.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Symmetry-based constructor diversity does change basins, but this particular seeded relabeling degrades the bundle more than it helps; arbitrary label-bias removal is not enough on its own.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert to the kept late repeat-guidance branch and avoid further arbitrary constructor-symmetry permutations for now. The next experiment should likely touch restart/breakout structure or a more principled constructor bias, not another relabeling trick.

description: Stopped writing random breakout swaps into the tabu history. That made breakout a true perturbation instead of an immediate short-term prohibition, and it improved the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: The kept late repeat-guidance branch may be over-constraining its own breakout recovery by immediately tabuing the two random breakout swaps; letting breakout be a pure perturbation could improve repairability without changing the neighborhood or benchmark semantics.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Removing breakout tabu recording materially improved the mean and lowered conflict-position tails while keeping solved-run count at 5; the hardest remaining bundle member is still 36x9x11 seed 2, but breakout now exits plateaus more effectively on the rest of the bundle.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout as an untabued perturbation. Next experiments should stay in this family of mild plateau-escape structure changes rather than adding more tie-break criteria or arbitrary constructor permutations.

description: Tried making the second breakout swap reactive by recomputing the conflict state after the first random swap and choosing the second swap from that updated state. It made breakout too deterministic and regressed the bundle.

asi.hypothesis: With breakout no longer tabued, perhaps the second random swap should respond to the first swap's new conflict landscape instead of sampling both swaps from the stale pre-breakout state.
asi.rollback_reason: Recomputing between the two breakout swaps regressed mean_final_score from 98.2 to 111.25 and dropped solved_runs from 5 to 2, so it over-shaped the perturbation instead of leaving it usefully noisy.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The successful breakout change seems to rely on keeping the perturbation lightly random; making the second swap reactive turned it into another guided operator and lost too much robustness.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout untabued but otherwise simple. Future plateau-escape experiments should preserve this light randomness rather than repeatedly recomputing or guiding the breakout path.

description: Tried spreading the two breakout swaps across distinct weeks when possible, instead of allowing both swaps to hit the same max-conflict week. This weakened the successful untabued breakout behavior and regressed the bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A mild plateau-escape refinement might help without making breakout more guided: keep breakout untabued, but spread its two random swaps across distinct weeks when multiple high-conflict weeks exist so the perturbation touches more than one local basin.
asi.rollback_reason: The distinct-weeks breakout variant regressed mean_final_score from 98.2 to 105.1 and worsened the hardest larger cases, especially 36x9x11 seeds 1, 2, and 4.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The successful untabued breakout seems to benefit from concentrating both random swaps where the search already sees the biggest weekly burden; forcing dispersion across weeks loses that advantage.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout untabued and allow concentration on the same hard week. Future plateau-escape ideas should avoid making the perturbation more structured along week boundaries.

description: Tried making breakout less self-similar by choosing one conflicted anchor slot from the max-conflict week and one random partner slot from a different group, instead of requiring both breakout slots to already be conflicted. This regressed the bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A milder breakout might preserve the gains from untabued perturbation while opening different repair paths: keep one breakout slot tied to a currently conflicted position, but make the partner slot random within another group so breakout is less self-reinforcing.
asi.rollback_reason: The anchored-plus-random breakout regressed mean_final_score from 98.2 to 105.75 and especially worsened the 36x9x11 tail, even though solved_runs stayed at 5.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The current breakout seems to work because both perturbation endpoints come from already-conflicted structure in the hard week; relaxing one endpoint to a random donor weakens repair quality more than it improves exploration.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout endpoint selection fully conflict-anchored. Avoid further breakout-slot randomization and instead look for other small post-breakout or stagnation-structure changes that preserve the successful concentrated perturbation.

description: Made the late active-repeated-pair guidance even more selective: it now activates only after 3+ non-improving iterations instead of 2+. This slightly improved the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: The late repeat-guidance keep may still be engaging a bit too early after the untabued breakout change; raising its activation threshold from 2 to 3 stagnant iterations could preserve the good basin escapes while reducing over-steering on medium-stagnation states.
asi.learned_tradeoff: A slightly later activation improves the mean and runtime, mainly by helping the harder larger p=4 cases, but it trades away one solved run versus the previous best. The primary metric still improved, so this is worth keeping.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this more selective >=3 threshold as the new baseline. Further experiments should continue to tune plateau-escape structure or late-only guidance carefully, with special attention to whether larger-case gains justify any loss in solved-run count.

description: Cleared the entire tabu history whenever breakout fires, on top of the existing untabued breakout perturbation. This was far too destructive and regressed the bundle badly.

asi.hypothesis: After delaying late repeat-guidance to 3+ stagnant iterations, perhaps the remaining self-blocking is old tabu residue surviving across breakouts; clearing tabu entirely on breakout might let the post-perturbation repair phase move more freely.
asi.rollback_reason: Global tabu clearing on breakout regressed mean_final_score from 97.2 to 112.75 and collapsed solved_runs from 4 to 0, indicating that some retained short-term memory is still crucial even with untabued breakout swaps.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Untabued breakout helps, but wiping all tabu state removes too much stabilizing structure and pushes the search into uniformly mediocre basins.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout untabued but retain ordinary tabu memory. Avoid global reset operators; future experiments should be smaller and more selective than full tabu clearing.

description: Cleared tabu only for the current max-conflict weeks when breakout fires, trying a selective post-breakout memory reset instead of a global one. This still regressed the bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A week-local version of the failed global reset might still help: only clear tabu entries for the current max-conflict weeks when breakout fires, leaving the rest of the short-term memory intact.
asi.rollback_reason: Even week-local tabu clearing regressed mean_final_score from 97.2 to 115.4 and cut solved_runs from 4 to 2, so post-breakout memory resets remain too destructive.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The current breakout already supplies enough perturbation; even selective tabu clearing removes too much repair structure on the hard weeks.
asi.next_action_hint: Abandon tabu-reset experiments for now. Keep breakout untabued but otherwise leave tabu history alone, and focus on smaller perturbation-quality changes such as avoiding redundant breakout swaps.

description: Made breakout swaps avoid reusing the same touched positions when possible. This prevents the two random breakout swaps from canceling or overlapping trivially, while keeping breakout untabued and otherwise simple. It improved the p=4 stretch bundle substantially.

asi.hypothesis: The untabued breakout may still waste perturbation budget because its two random swaps can overlap or partially undo each other. Requiring the two breakout swaps to touch distinct positions when possible could strengthen plateau escape without making it more guided.
asi.learned_tradeoff: This is a strong generic improvement: keeping breakout swaps distinct improved both the hard 36x9x11 tails and solved-run count, while preserving the simple untabued conflict-anchored breakout structure.
asi.next_action_hint: Use distinct-position untabued breakout as the new baseline. Future breakout work should keep this non-overlap property and look for similarly simple perturbation-efficiency improvements rather than stronger guidance or resets.

description: Tried strengthening breakout efficiency further by avoiding reuse of the same groups when both breakout swaps hit the same week, on top of the existing distinct-position rule. This regressed the p=4 stretch bundle badly.

asi.hypothesis: The next mild breakout-efficiency step was to avoid reusing the same groups when both breakout swaps hit the same week, so the perturbation would spread across four groups instead of clustering into two or three.
asi.rollback_reason: This over-structured breakout. mean_final_score regressed from 91.25 to 115.35, and the hard 36x9x11 tails blew up to 430 on two seeds.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Distinct-position breakout was helpful, but adding a same-week distinct-group requirement pushed the perturbation too far toward forced spreading. Breakout still wants to stay concentrated on the hard structure rather than explicitly covering more groups.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit same-week group-spreading for breakout. Keep the distinct-position rule but otherwise preserve concentrated breakout, and look for milder ideas outside explicit spread constraints.

description: Tried making late active-repeated-pair guidance even more selective by delaying it from 3+ to 4+ non-improving iterations. This softened the guidance too much and regressed the bundle versus the current best.

asi.hypothesis: The current late repeat-guidance may still be slightly too eager. Delaying activation from 3+ to 4+ non-improving iterations could keep the useful signal only for deeper plateaus.
asi.rollback_reason: The stricter threshold regressed mean_final_score from 91.25 to 97.9. The current 3+ activation remains the better late-stage balance.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Late repeat-guidance helps only in a narrow window: 2+ was too early, 4+ is too late, and 3+ remains the current best threshold.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing 3+ threshold. If guidance is revisited again, avoid moving the activation later; look for different mild, generic ideas rather than more threshold pushing.

description: Tried a principled best-of-two greedy initializer: build two independent randomized greedy schedules and start local search from the one with the better internal paper metrics. This hurt badly, collapsing all 32x8x10 seeds to 66.

asi.hypothesis: A generic mini-portfolio in the initializer might help: generate two independent greedy candidates and start local search from the one with the lower internal paper-style conflict metrics.
asi.rollback_reason: This selection pressure overfit the initializer stage and destroyed useful basin diversity. mean_final_score regressed from 91.25 to 112.8, with all 32x8x10 seeds stuck at 66.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Choosing the apparently better greedy construction by local paper metrics is not actually a good proxy for downstream search basin quality. The current solver benefits from keeping the single sampled construction rather than screening it.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit best-of-two initializer screening with current paper metrics. Future constructor work should preserve basin diversity instead of pre-filtering candidates by shallow local quality.

description: Tried delaying breakout from 4 to 5 non-improving iterations so the existing 3+ late repeat-guidance would get more time before perturbation. This improved some guardrail cases but regressed the primary bundle versus the current best.

asi.hypothesis: The current 3+ late repeat-guidance may not have enough time to work before breakout fires. Delaying breakout from 4 to 5 non-improving iterations could preserve the same guidance rule while giving it an extra chance to repair the plateau.
asi.rollback_reason: This tradeoff was not good enough for the primary metric: mean_final_score regressed from 91.25 to 99.0, and only one 32x8x10 seed solved instead of two.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Giving late guidance more runway before breakout helped some guardrail cases but weakened the primary 32x8x10 lane. The current breakout timing is still part of the best overall balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout timing at the current baseline. Future experiments should move away from timing-only scalar tweaks and toward different mild mechanisms, while preserving the simple untabued conflict-anchored distinct-position breakout.

description: Tried reordering the existing late-stage tie-breaks so max-conflict-week concentration stays ahead of active-repeated-pair guidance when the late guidance is active. This modestly improved some larger-case behavior but still regressed the primary bundle versus the current best.

asi.hypothesis: The current late active-repeated-pair guidance may be slightly over-prioritizing pair-count cleanup over conflict concentration. Keeping the existing max-conflict-week tie-break ahead of active_repeated_pairs_after could preserve the current signals while concentrating repairs better.
asi.rollback_reason: The reordered precedence improved some guardrail metrics but still regressed the primary mean_final_score from 91.25 to 95.0, so the current tie-break order remains better overall.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The existing late repeat-guidance ordering is already close to the best balance. Reordering the same signals can help larger cases but still harms the primary 32x8x10 objective.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop tweaking ordering among the current late-stage signals for now. The next experiment should try a different mild mechanism, likely around how stagnation is counted or how tabu interacts with non-improving local-search moves, without broadening breakout or constructor filtering.

description: Counted stagnation against best-so-far improvements instead of any current-step conflict improvement. This lets late repeat-guidance and breakout stay active while the search is still circling below the incumbent best basin, and it produced a major bundle improvement.

asi.hypothesis: The stagnation counter may be too forgiving because it resets on any current-step conflict improvement, even when the search is still below the incumbent best. Resetting only on best-so-far improvement could let the existing late guidance and breakout engage sooner on false-progress plateaus.
asi.learned_tradeoff: This is a strong generic improvement. The solver benefits from treating below-best recovery moves as continued stagnation, which preserves pressure for late guidance and breakout instead of over-crediting local downhill motion.
asi.next_action_hint: Use best-so-far stagnation counting as the new baseline. Future experiments can build on this plateau semantics, but avoid reintroducing stale scalar threshold changes or constructor screening.

description: Tried a softer stagnation rule: best-so-far improvements still reset the counter, but current-step improvements below the incumbent only decay the counter by one instead of leaving it unchanged. This was too soft and regressed the bundle.

asi.hypothesis: Best-so-far stagnation counting helped a lot, but maybe full persistence through below-best recovery moves is slightly too aggressive. A partial decay on current-step improvement could preserve most of the pressure while relaxing it a bit during real descent.
asi.rollback_reason: That relaxation was too soft. mean_final_score regressed from 84.8 to 106.1 and solved_runs fell from 6 to 3.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The new baseline needs the full best-so-far stagnation pressure. Even mild forgiveness for below-best recovery moves weakens the late guidance/breakout regime too much.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep strict best-so-far stagnation counting. Future experiments should leave this counter semantics intact and look elsewhere, likely at tabu interaction or other mild plateau-control mechanisms.

description: Tried a broader tabu aspiration rule: allow tabu moves when they improve the current schedule even if they do not beat the global best. This badly weakened the bundle, especially larger cases.

asi.hypothesis: With the new best-so-far stagnation semantics, tabu aspiration may still be too strict. Allowing tabu moves that improve the current schedule, even if they do not beat the global best, could help basin recovery under the stronger plateau pressure.
asi.rollback_reason: This weakened tabu too much. mean_final_score regressed from 84.8 to 118.05, and larger cases degraded sharply.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The new baseline needs strict tabu aspiration. Best-so-far stagnation pressure already increases search aggressiveness; broadening aspiration on top of that destabilizes larger-case search.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep tabu aspiration strict. Do not loosen it beyond best-beating moves. Future experiments should keep the new stagnation semantics but avoid broad tabu weakening.

description: Retuned late active-repeated-pair guidance for the new best-so-far stagnation semantics by delaying activation from 3+ to 4+ non-improving iterations. Under the stricter stagnation counter this reduced over-eager guidance and improved the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: Best-so-far stagnation counting made the old 3+ late repeat-guidance threshold effectively stronger. Delaying the guidance to 4+ non-improving iterations might keep the new plateau semantics while reducing over-eager tie-break steering.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Threshold tuning was stale under the old current-step-reset regime, but under best-so-far stagnation semantics a slightly later repeat-guidance trigger is beneficial. The new plateau pressure stays strong while avoiding premature tie-break steering.
asi.next_action_hint: Use 4+ late repeat-guidance as the new baseline together with best-so-far stagnation counting. Future experiments can consider whether breakout timing should be retuned under the same new semantics, but avoid constructor screening and broad tabu weakening.

description: Retuned breakout timing under the new best-so-far stagnation semantics and 4+ late repeat-guidance threshold by delaying breakout from 4 to 5 non-improving iterations. This increased solved runs on guardrails but regressed the primary bundle versus the current best.

asi.hypothesis: With best-so-far stagnation counting and the delayed 4+ repeat-guidance trigger, breakout at 4 may now be slightly too eager. Pushing breakout to 5 could let the milder guidance act once before perturbation.
asi.rollback_reason: This helped some guardrails and increased solved_runs, but primary mean_final_score regressed from 81.7 to 89.25, so breakout stayed too delayed for the main objective.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Under the new stagnation semantics, raising the repeat-guidance threshold to 4 helped, but delaying breakout to 5 over-favors guardrail polishing at the expense of the primary bundle. Breakout at 4 still appears to be the better overall balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the new 4+ repeat-guidance threshold but restore breakout at 4. Future experiments should explore different mild mechanisms under the new plateau semantics rather than more breakout-delay tuning.

description: Retuned solver4's week-local pair tabu tenure from 10 iterations down to 8 under the new best-so-far stagnation regime. Shorter tabu reduced some blocking, but regressed the bundle versus the current best.

asi.hypothesis: With best-so-far stagnation counting and 4+ late repeat-guidance, the fixed week-local tabu tenure of 10 might now be slightly too conservative. Reducing it to 8 could release productive revisits sooner without broadening aspiration.
asi.rollback_reason: Shortening tabu was regressive: mean_final_score worsened from 81.7 to 86.8, with weaker 36x9x11 performance and only 7 solved runs.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The new baseline still benefits from the stronger tabu memory. Best-so-far stagnation pressure does not mean the tabu tenure should be shortened; doing so weakens hard-case search stability.
asi.next_action_hint: Restore tabu tenure 10. If tabu is revisited, try the opposite direction or a more conditional mechanism instead of further shortening.

description: Increased solver4's week-local pair tabu tenure from 10 to 12 iterations under the best-so-far stagnation + 4+ late repeat-guidance regime. The stronger short-term memory improved the p=4 stretch bundle, especially larger hard cases.

asi.hypothesis: Shortening tabu tenure regressed, suggesting the new best-so-far stagnation regime still benefits from stronger short-term memory. Increasing the fixed week-local tabu tenure from 10 to 12 might stabilize hard-case search without changing guidance or breakout structure.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Under best-so-far stagnation counting, the solver prefers stronger tabu memory, not weaker. A slightly longer tenure improved larger-case stability while preserving the perfect 32x8x10 result.
asi.next_action_hint: Use tabu tenure 12 as the new baseline. If plateau-control is explored further, prefer similarly mild strengthening/conditioning of existing memory rather than loosening aspiration or shortening tenure.

description: Tried strengthening solver4's fixed week-local pair tabu tenure further from 12 to 14 iterations under the best-so-far stagnation + 4+ late repeat-guidance regime. The extra memory overconstrained search and regressed the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: Since moving tabu tenure from 10 to 12 helped, a further mild increase to 14 might continue the stronger-memory trend without disturbing the effective best-so-far stagnation, late repeat-guidance, or breakout structure.
asi.rollback_reason: The stronger fixed tenure overconstrained search. mean_final_score regressed from 79.4 to 86.55, with larger-case regressions outweighing the extra solved run.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The stronger-memory direction is real only up to a point. Tenure 12 helped over 10, but 14 overshot; the live sweet spot appears narrower than 'keep increasing tabu'.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep 12 as the fixed baseline. Prune the idea of further unconditional tenure increases and look for a different mild mechanism or a conditional memory scheme rather than a larger global tabu constant.

description: Tried a conditional stronger-memory scheme: keep solver4's base week-local pair tabu tenure at 12, but extend recorded swaps on the current max-conflict week(s) to tenure 14. This over-targeted hard weeks and regressed the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A fixed global tenure of 14 was too much, but perhaps only the current max-conflict week needs extra short-term memory. Recording swaps on the hardest week(s) with tenure 14 while leaving other weeks at 12 might stabilize the hard basin without globally overconstraining search.
asi.rollback_reason: This conditional longer-memory scheme still oversteered the search and regressed mean_final_score from 79.4 to 93.0, especially on 36x9x10 and 40x10x11.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Even conditional extra tabu on the hardest week is too much. The winning tenure-12 result does not seem to want additional memory layered specifically onto conflict hotspots.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep fixed tenure 12 and prune both unconditional and max-conflict-week tenure increases. Look for a different mild mechanism outside the tabu-memory axis.

description: Tried making breakout gentler by reducing solver4's random breakout from two swaps to one while keeping the same best-so-far stagnation, untabued breakout, and fixed-tenure-12 regime. The smaller perturbation was too weak and regressed the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: The current two-swap breakout may be slightly too disruptive under the stronger fixed-tenure-12 baseline. Reducing breakout to a single untabued, conflict-anchored swap might preserve more good structure while still escaping plateaus.
asi.rollback_reason: A one-swap breakout was too weak. mean_final_score regressed from 79.4 to 89.95 and 32x8x10 lost two perfect seeds.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The current breakout needs more than one swap to escape the hard basin honestly. The successful breakout operator is simple, but not that gentle.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout at two swaps. If breakout strength is revisited again, do not weaken it below the current baseline; any further breakout experiments should preserve at least the current perturbation strength.

description: Tried strengthening breakout slightly by increasing solver4's untabued conflict-anchored breakout from two swaps to three while keeping fixed tenure 12 and the same stagnation regime. The stronger perturbation still regressed the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A one-swap breakout was too weak, so perhaps the current baseline wants a slightly stronger but still simple perturbation. Increasing breakout from two swaps to three might improve hard-case escape while preserving the same structure and honesty constraints.
asi.rollback_reason: The stronger perturbation still regressed mean_final_score from 79.4 to 87.0, so the current two-swap breakout remains the best balance.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The breakout-strength axis appears locally tuned already: one swap is too weak, three swaps is too strong, and the current two-swap perturbation remains the best simple setting.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep breakout at two swaps and stop tuning simple breakout strength. The next experiment should move to a different mild mechanism outside straightforward tabu or breakout-strength changes.

description: Tried narrowing late active-repeated-pair guidance so it only activates after 4+ stagnant iterations when the current schedule is exactly at the incumbent best conflict-position level, instead of also during below-best recovery states. This was milder than the baseline but regressed the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: Under best-so-far stagnation counting, active-repeated-pair guidance may help mainly when the search is truly plateaued at the incumbent best, not while it is still recovering below best. Restricting the late guidance to current==best might reduce oversteer while keeping the stronger stagnation semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: The narrower guidance was too weak. mean_final_score regressed from 79.4 to 83.05, mainly by worsening 36x9x10 while leaving the main hard 36x9x11 behavior essentially unchanged.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The kept 4+ repeat-guidance seems to provide useful steering even during below-best recovery states. Restricting it to exact incumbent-best plateaus removes too much of that benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing 4+ repeat-guidance semantics. Future work should look for a different mild mechanism rather than narrowing its state gating further.

description: Tried reintroducing late active-repeated-pair guidance in a much narrower form: activate it only from the pre-breakout stagnation step onward and only when the current schedule is already at the incumbent best conflict-position level. A confirmation rerun reproduced the same 80.6 mean_final_score, so the small regression appears real.

asi.hypothesis: Because breakout fires at the same stagnation depth as the current 4+ repeat-guidance threshold, a narrower reintroduction might work: enable repeat guidance only on the final pre-breakout local-search step and only when the search is already sitting at the incumbent-best conflict plateau.
asi.rollback_reason: The narrowed reintroduction still regressed the primary metric. A confirmation rerun reproduced mean_final_score 80.6 exactly, so this is not just measurement noise.
asi.learned_tradeoff: The best current regime still prefers effectively no extra active-repeated-pair steering beyond the kept baseline semantics. Even a very narrow pre-breakout/incumbent-best version slightly worsened the bundle.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune repeat-guidance reintroduction ideas for now. Look next at a different mild move-ordering or neighborhood idea that does not revisit the tabu-memory axis, breakout strength axis, or active-repeated-pair guidance thresholds.

description: Tried a mild late-stage move-ordering tweak: on the last pre-breakout local-search step, break otherwise-equal swap ties toward moves taken from the currently harder week instead of falling straight to lexicographic order. This over-focused on hard-week structure and regressed the p=4 stretch bundle.

asi.hypothesis: With simple tabu and breakout-strength tuning exhausted, a mild remaining option was to add hard-week-aware move ordering: on the last local-search step before breakout, prefer otherwise-equal swaps drawn from the currently hardest week to concentrate effort where the schedule is most conflicted.
asi.rollback_reason: This overfocused the search on hard-week structure and regressed mean_final_score from 79.4 to 88.55, especially on 36x9x11 and one 40x10x11 seed.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Even very mild move-order bias toward the current hardest week can oversteer larger hard cases. The current baseline likely needs broader flexibility than 'prefer the hardest week' at tie time.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune hard-week tie-focus for now. The next experiment should target a different mild neighborhood-shaping idea rather than more hard-week-only ordering or further simple plateau-control retunes.

description: Tried a late-stage current-week-resolution tie-break: on the last pre-breakout local-search step, break otherwise-equal swaps by lower conflict count in the move's own week after the swap, ahead of lexicographic order. A confirmation rerun reproduced the same 85.6 mean_final_score, so this week-local tie focus appears genuinely worse than baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A milder neighborhood-shaping step than hard-week source bias is to favor moves that actually reduce conflict inside their own week after the swap. Activating that only on the final pre-breakout step might help clean up hard local structure without changing early search behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: This week-local tie focus still regressed the bundle. A confirmation rerun reproduced mean_final_score 85.6, so the result appears real rather than noise.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Late-stage tie-breaks that further concentrate pressure on one week's structure appear to oversteer the larger hard cases, even when they are based on realized post-swap improvement rather than raw source-week burden.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune late-stage week-local tie-focus ideas for now. Future experiments should try a different kind of late-stage neighborhood expansion rather than additional tie-break pressure on the existing candidate set.

description: Tried a limited late-stage neighborhood expansion: on the last pre-breakout step, allow otherwise clean-clean swaps from the currently hardest week instead of restricting consideration to swaps with at least one conflicted endpoint. A confirmation rerun improved over the first attempt but still regressed the p=4 stretch bundle versus baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Instead of adding more tie-break pressure, a better mild direction might be to expand the candidate neighborhood only after real stagnation. Allowing clean-clean swaps from the hardest week on the final pre-breakout step could expose escape moves that the normal conflict-filtered neighborhood misses.
asi.rollback_reason: The expanded neighborhood remained weaker than baseline. A confirmation rerun improved over the first attempt but still landed at mean_final_score 81.95 versus the 79.4 baseline.
asi.learned_tradeoff: Late-stage neighborhood expansion around the hardest week is less damaging than late-stage week-focused tie-breaking, but it still over-emphasizes generic week pressure and does not beat the current baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune simplest hard-week-only neighborhood expansion. If neighborhood shaping is revisited, pivot toward repeated-pair-structured expansion rather than generic hardest-week expansion.

description: Tried a mild repeated-pair-structured neighborhood expansion: on the final pre-breakout stagnation step, allow clean-clean swaps only when both endpoints belong to groups that already contain repeated-pair conflict. The simple group-level expansion remained worse than the baseline, regressing the expanded p=4 bundle despite being less damaging than earlier hard-week-only expansion variants.
